explanations.13: TextEncoder converts a string into raw bytes, which is necessary because Bluesky uses byte positions (not character positions) to mark where links and special text formatting start and end.
explanations.14: TextDecoder does the opposite—it converts bytes back into readable text, which we need when we've sliced the byte array into pieces.
explanations.27: The find() method searches through the facet's features array and returns the first feature that matches the condition (in this case, one marked as a link).
explanations.97: The split('/').pop() extracts the last segment from a URL-like URI string, which is the unique record ID needed to build links to this specific Bluesky post.
explanations.111: This uses a template literal to dynamically calculate left padding based on reply.depth, making nested replies indent deeper on the page.
explanations.208: This defines a helper function inside the useEffect that recursively flattens a tree of nested replies into a single array, tracking how deep each reply is nested.
explanations.212: When a reply has its own replies, this line calls the function recursively with those nested replies and increments the depth counter.
